fact: November 24 is exactly 33 days before the winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere - that's like counting down from your birthday to Christmas!
In Alaska, the sun rises after 9 AM and sets before 4 PM on this date, giving kids less than 7 hours of daylight to play outside.
Deer in North America are typically in the middle of their rutting season around November 24, which means male deer are acting super dramatic and showing off for females.
The zodiac sign switches from Scorpio to Sagittarius right around this time - November 24 babies are fire-sign Sagittarians who love adventure!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 24 feels like late spring heading into summer - perfect beach weather while the north gets chilly.
Maple trees in cold regions have usually completely dropped their leaves by this date, creating crunchy carpets that make satisfying sounds when you stomp through them.
The moon phase on November 24 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it appears extra bright because the air is clearer in late autumn.
Squirrels are in full panic mode around this time, frantically burying nuts and seeds before winter hits - they can bury up to 10,000 nuts in a season!
In northern countries, the first hard frost that kills most remaining plants often happens around November 24, turning gardens into sparkly ice sculptures overnight.
November 24 falls exactly in the 48th week of the year - almost at the finish line of the calendar!
Geese flying south create their famous V-formations most actively around this date, and the lead goose changes every few minutes because flying in front is exhausting.
The sun sets with more orange and red colors in late November because sunlight travels through more atmosphere when the sun sits lower in the sky.
